name: LEGACY HOI AN RESORT
detail: I stayed here for 2 nights. It was the first trip with my mom. So I was concerned about room condition and service of a hotel very much. Ancient house village has a beautiful garden and very cozy and clean bedroom. We are really satisfied. One day, I and my mom waited the hotel shuttle bus nearby the acient town but we couldn't find the bus stop. we thought that we waited at the right address on the map the hotel gave us. But we missed the bus. We came back to the resort by Grab and asked about the suttle bus. And it was found to be our misunderstading. But the staff kindly checked and explained all situation. Plus, she suggested that they will help us to move to the another hotel on the next day. (We booked another hotel in Hoi An for 2 more nights) Especially, the manager Anh (I think she is the general manager of Acient house resort) checked our schedule every single morning and suggested good ways to enjoy our trip. She also knew about our situation(the shuttle bus episode) As if she was my sister, she felt sorry for me. I could feel her sincerity and I was very impressed about it. Also, I was really grateful to Anh. I hope she would have happy days and be healthy! Plus, every staff of the Acient house resort was very kind. And the chef of restaruant have a great skill. If I come to visit Hoi An again, I will definitely book Acient house village. Thank you! It is the first time I write the comment in Agora this long! Lol Thanks from Korea!
area: Cam Thanh,Hoi An - View on map

name: La Siesta Hoi An Resort & Spa
detail: This is my third stay at a La Siesta property and the first time in Hoi An. I continue to be impressed by this chain’s high standards in hospitality and professionalism, particularly when dealing with an issue we had - not with the hotel but with one of the restaurants they recommended. My friends and I will continue staying at La Siesta properties when in Vietnam.

name: Hoi An Canal House Riverside by Haviland
detail: Stayed here for 5 nights in May. Price was very reasonable, breakfast was fresh and delicious, staff very friendly. We requested a corner room when we booked online and they were larger than the river-facing rooms. But regardless the rooms are stylish and the Super-King bed is among the most comfortable I have ever slept in! Ellie at the front desk did an amazing job checking us in, greeting us by name everyday, and sending us off. Breakfast is ordered the night before and served quickly when you sit down. My boyfriend loved the local noodle dishes and the fruit was great. Bikes are available for free and the hotel is the perfect distance from the ancient town to enjoy he river-side ride. All bikes are new and in great condition compared to many cruisers we have rented in Asia. The pool was better to enjoy at night with the color changing lights and less traffic or construction noise - all of which were never a bother in the room with high quality windows that block out all outdoor noise. Definitely would stay again! We loved Hoi An, one of our favorite destinations in Vietnam!
area: Cẩm Phô,Hoi An - View on map

name: Lasenta Boutique Hotel Hoian
detail: Our stay in Hoi An was overall an excellent experience. Lasenta itself is a very new hotel (3 months from what the concierge told us) and it definitely shows. Upon arriving, we were given a cold towel and a cool herbal drink and then were given the run down of information before being taken to our room. The concierge were always attending to our needs whether it's calling for a taxi or helping with our luggage or just greeting in the mornings. Our room (Grand Deluxe) was plenty spacious, the bed very large and comfy and overall the room was very clean. The view from the room was breath taking as we had the rice fields view and waking up to that every morning was bliss. The WIFI in the room was a little spotty (we were located on the 6th floor) and seemed rather slow unless we sat near towards the door (which is most likely closer to the access point). However, WIFI in the lobby and restaurant were extremely fast (around ~10Mbps) so they should probably improve on the signal in the rooms. Since breakfast was bundled with our room, the breakfast is your standard fare - generally sausage, bacon, beans, mash potato, potato tarts, curry, congee, breads, fruits, cold meats and cheese. They also have a station where two chefs will make your eggs to your preference and one to prepare your bowl of Pho. Food quality, it's rather decent, I actually have no complaints about it. The hotel has various facilities such as the infinity-esque pool (great for taking pictures) and also a gym, which is equipped with two treadmills, 1 crosstrainer, a lat-pulldown, bench press and free weights. I managed to get 3 decent workouts with the provided equipment. Location wise, I would say very good as it is not in the heart of the bustling and tourist heavy Hoi An town but also not that far that it is very inconvenient. Walking from the hotel to Hoi An Ancient Town took about 15-20 minutes. There's restaurants just 5 minute walk behind the hotel (I recommend Wooden House, the food there
